_meta.description: Friend & Cook canonical models enriched with decision-ready fields for bilingual co-teaching.

models.name: One Teach, One Observe
models.overview: The first teacher teaches the lesson, while the second teacher is making specific and detailed observations. These preplanned and purposeful observations may include academic, behaviors, or social skills.
models.classroom_setup: The first teacher is at the front of the room, where all students have visibility. The second teacher may be stationed somewhere unobtrusive, or may be roaming to make observations.
advantages: Teachers gain valuable data from the observations to guide future instruction.
disadvantages: May cause students to view the second teacher as just an aide.
If used too often, may lead to resentment by the first teacher for always leading the instruction.
best_for: Limited periods of time when something specific needs to be observed
When one teacher has a stronger content knowledge of the particular skill being taught
when_to_use: High-stakes observation of language use patterns
when_to_avoid: When parity is a priority for student perception

models.name: One Teach, One Assist
models.overview: One teacher teaches the lesson, while the second teacher is roaming, helping students as needed or managing behaviors.
models.classroom_setup: The first teacher is at the front of the room, where all students have visibility. The second teacher is roaming to assist individual students.
advantages: Allows the first teacher to teach uninterrupted.
disadvantages: May place the role of the second teacher as a bad cop and cause resentment among students, if they spend the majority of their time managing behavior.
best_for: Short periods of time when one teacher has stronger expertise in the subject matter
When some students will need lots of individual guidance
when_to_avoid: For extended units without parity rotations

teacher_roles.bilingual: roams to scaffold language and behavior

models.name: Parallel Teaching
models.overview: The class is divided into two groups, and each teacher delivers the same instruction to half of the class.
models.classroom_setup: The room is divided into two distinct sections, with one teacher in each section with half the class.
advantages: Students benefit from smaller groups, and groups can be strategically planned (and changed frequently) based on readiness levels.
disadvantages: Two simultaneous groups could be distracting if there is not enough separation.
Timing will need to be carefully monitored.
best_for: Frequent use when students will benefit from a smaller group and more individualized attention
When teachers have equally strong content knowledge
when_to_use: Ratio reduction for oral language practice
when_to_avoid: In cramped spaces with poor acoustics
When teachers' content expertise differs substantially

teacher_roles.bilingual: leads the other with language scaffolds

models.name: Station Teaching
models.overview: Students and content are divided into three groups. Each teacher teaches one section of content, and the third section is based on independent practice activities. Students rotate between all 3 groups.
models.classroom_setup: Three distinct stations are set up, one for each teacher (might be a carpet space, small group table, or just desks arranged in front of the board) and one for independent student work.
advantages: Smaller groups means more individual student attention.
Teachers can plan their lesson based on their strengths.
disadvantages: Timing is critical and may be hard to coordinate perfectly.
Some students may not be able to manage themselves appropriately in the independent station.
Requires a great amount of planning.

teacher_roles.bilingual: leads language-focused station; manages L1 scaffolds

models.name: Alternative Teaching
models.overview: One teacher teaches a lesson to the majority of the class, while a second teacher pulls a small group for an alternate or modified lesson.
models.classroom_setup: The first teacher is at the front of the room leading the instruction, while the second teacher meets with a small group in a separate area.
advantages: Provides an opportunity for enrichment or intervention as needed.
best_for: Times when your data indicates that a small group of students needs extra assistance before moving on
Deeper enrichment while the majority of the class needs reteaching
when_to_avoid: If small group pull-outs stigmatize students

teacher_roles.bilingual: leads small group with L1/L2 supports

models.name: Team Teaching
models.overview: Both teachers are fully involved in delivering the instruction at the same time.
models.classroom_setup: Both teachers are at the front of the room or roaming as needed as they deliver instruction.
advantages: Allows students to see two perspectives of a lesson / topic.
Models a strong professional relationship between two adults.
disadvantages: Requires a great amount of planning and coordination.
best_for: As often as feasible based on the amount of planning required
When students will benefit from two viewpoints, or two strategies, to learn the content
when_to_use: Co-modeling sentence frames and talk moves
when_to_avoid: When co-planning time is very limited
For tightly scripted test-prep with single-voice delivery

teacher_roles.bilingual: co-leads language scaffolds and L1 connections
